proposed_from.topic: Joint two-sided Rosenbaum sensitivity calibration from a shared least-favorable assignment law (bridge: matched-study Gamma-sensitivity x minimax multiple testing), using the banked stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax_stat result only as substrate: construct a computable level-indexed primal-dual calibration for heterogeneous matched sets that is operational without a known-Q oracle; prove finite-sample validity against every Gamma-bounded assignment law; characterize the exact (alpha,Gamma,design) region where the joint two-sided sensitivity bound strictly improves the conventional Bonferroni-doubled pair of one-sided bounds, including exact equality at Gamma=1 and the verified strict gap 0.158 at Gamma=2; prove when the least-favorable support switches and give an algorithm/certificate usable by sensitivitymv and senfm and by two-sided sensitivity reporting. The field-level headline must be the implementable joint procedure plus its strict-improvement theorem and published-workflow comparison; the banked finite all-tests LP, pair NP theorem, support-switch examples, and oracle design-sensitivity ceiling are lemmas and must not be resubmitted as the novelty claim.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: The theorem-level workflow comparators are Rosenbaum's one-sided \(M\)-statistic analysis and Zhao's pair-matched doubled-smaller-tail sensitivity-value analysis. The open question is a strict tightening because it asks for the exact shared-law union calibration and a certificate that can reject whenever \(p_J\le\alpha<p_B\), including heterogeneous matched sets outside Zhao's pair-matched asymptotics. Fogarty--Small, Rosenbaum (2018), Cohen--Olson--Fogarty, and robust-testing work are method ancestors only. The finite validity argument, the \(\Gamma=1\) equality, and the \(19/120\) witness are explicitly credited substrate rather than claimed novelty.
seed_list: Joint union-tail randomization p-value from one shared Rosenbaum law.
Critical-\(\Gamma\) frontier for strict joint improvement over doubled tails.
Support-switch limit law for the joint sensitivity value.
Efficiency bound for a repeated-design shared-law sensitivity functional.
Minimax complexity lower bound for exact heterogeneous-set two-tail calibration.
Policy-style allocation of two-sided error budget under hidden assignment bias.
Characterize the incompatible directional adversaries implicit in Bonferroni doubling and correct them by a shared-law p-value.
Primal-dual support-map algorithm for least-favorable two-tail Rosenbaum laws.

seed_details.seed: Joint union-tail randomization p-value from one shared Rosenbaum law.
seed_details.anchor_read: Abstract and introduction identify the incompatible assignment mechanisms induced by separate analyses; the paper's conclusion operationalizes a shared-law QCQP across outcomes.
seed_details.red_team: Immediate from a union bound because two-sided testing is only two one-sided tests.
seed_details.rebuttal: The union probability is maximized over one law, while Bonferroni separately maximizes each tail; the \(19/120\) witness separates the objects.

seed_details.seed: Critical-\(\Gamma\) frontier for strict joint improvement over doubled tails.
seed_details.anchor_read: The available record and gap context identify the operational one-sided \(M\)-test workflow and its routine two-sided doubling.
seed_details.red_team: The threshold depends on a fully specified alternative and is not an inference result.
seed_details.rebuttal: A finite p-value strictness cell is defined wholly by observed scores and the null assignment polytope, not by an alternative law.

seed_details.seed: Support-switch limit law for the joint sensitivity value.
seed_details.anchor_read: The abstract develops a nonasymptotic adaptive paired test and its design sensitivity; its scope is paired data.
seed_details.red_team: Nonsmooth support switches defeat a uniform limit law.
seed_details.rebuttal: A margin-away-from-switch regime could restore regularity, but no primitive margin theorem was located.

seed_details.seed: Efficiency bound for a repeated-design shared-law sensitivity functional.
seed_details.anchor_read: Introduction and Sections 3--4 formulate a coherent one-sided game and derive an asymptotic chi-bar-squared calibration; discussion leaves difficult extensions open.
seed_details.red_team: There is no regular superpopulation estimand because the target is a finite conditional p-value.
seed_details.rebuttal: No natural regular parameterization was found, so this is not pursued.

seed_details.seed: Minimax complexity lower bound for exact heterogeneous-set two-tail calibration.
seed_details.anchor_read: The introduction poses the absence of feasible arbitrary-outcome heterogeneous-stratum bounds; the conclusion emphasizes conservative computational tractability.
seed_details.red_team: A complexity reduction would not itself improve causal inference.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is useful only as a diagnostic for the algorithmic seed, not as the main contribution.

seed_details.seed: Policy-style allocation of two-sided error budget under hidden assignment bias.
seed_details.anchor_read: The paper's analyst-versus-adversary formulation motivates optimized choices against a shared nuisance law.
seed_details.red_team: Tail allocation is not treatment-policy learning and has no welfare target.
seed_details.rebuttal: The required policy-regret object is absent; this lane is excluded by the literature and target geometry.

seed_details.seed: Characterize the incompatible directional adversaries implicit in Bonferroni doubling and correct them by a shared-law p-value.
seed_details.anchor_read: The introduction states that separate analyses permit an implausibly outcome-specific assignment mechanism, and the conclusion demonstrates gains from enforcing a common law.
seed_details.red_team: This merely restates the multiple-outcome QCQP.
seed_details.rebuttal: Opposite tails of one statistic require an exact finite union-tail calibration and a support-switch map, neither supplied by that QCQP.

seed_details.seed: Primal-dual support-map algorithm for least-favorable two-tail Rosenbaum laws.
seed_details.anchor_read: The introduction and conclusion explain that least-favorable distributions govern minimax detectors and that multiple-hypothesis robust testing is fundamentally distinct from binary testing.
seed_details.red_team: Column generation is generic optimization with no statistical novelty.
seed_details.rebuttal: The required separation, certificate, and strictness cells are specialized to the Rosenbaum product-law geometry and observable causal statistic.

proposed_from.literature_map: Rosenbaum (1987) supplies the bounded-odds conditional randomization model. Rosenbaum (2007) supplies the selected-statistic one-sided \(M\)-test workflow used in matched-study software. Fogarty--Small (2016) enforce common hidden-assignment probabilities across outcomes but do not calibrate opposite tails of one statistic. Rosenbaum (2018) gives the closest heterogeneous-set operational method, with a conservative Taylor bound rather than an exact shared union-tail calculation. Cohen--Olson--Fogarty (2020) solve an asymptotic directional adversarial game over coherent statistic combinations, not an exact finite two-tail p-value. Howard--Pimentel (2021) deliver nonasymptotic adaptive paired signed-rank sensitivity testing. Fauß--Zoubir--Poor (2021) provide least-favorable-distribution and saddle-point ideas but no Rosenbaum product-law support certificate. The settled literature is selected-statistic or directional shared-law inference; the unresolved kernel is observable finite two-tail shared-law calibration with exact support geometry.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Fisher-sharp-null calibration
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: All reported p-value validity and any certified calibration are conditional on the Fisher sharp null and the conditional Rosenbaum product-law model.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Exact shared-law two-tail calibration under the Fisher-sharp-null conditional Rosenbaum model.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Encoded-input computational regime
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The computational target is limited to finite rational scores, threshold, alpha, and Gamma and makes no polynomial-time claim.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Conditional exact arithmetic on the declared finite encoded-input domain, with potentially exponential runtime.

banked_reason: V6 reviewer assessed the sound capability and hardness kernel at letter tier; field advancement requires a new machine-model complexity or statistical-performance theorem.
